en.name: Oxygen partial pressure in venous blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context The venous partial pressure of oxygen (PvO2) is the pressure of O2 dissolved in venous blood, after tissue oxygen extraction. In healthy adults, mixed venous PO2 is ~ 40 mmHg, much lower than arterial PO2 (~ 80-100 mmHg under room air) [1]. PvO2 is used in intensive care to assess the adequacy of oxygen delivery relative to consumption, mainly via the venous oxygen saturation derived from it. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by a blood gas analyser on a venous sample. Results are reported in mmHg (US convention) or kPa (SI / European convention) [2]. **Note: arterial vs venous.** This concept set covers **venous PO2 only** (peripheral venous, central venous, mixed venous, capillary blood). Arterial PO2 is captured by the separate concept set "Oxygen partial pressure in arterial blood". **Note: venous specimens.** Central and mixed venous PO2 are most clinically relevant in critical care; peripheral venous PO2 is highly dependent on tourniquet, local perfusion and metabolism. The user should preserve the specimen distinction in analyses where the source-of-blood matters. **Note: temperature-corrected variant.** Modern blood gas analysers measure at 37 °C. A separate LOINC code exists for PO2 corrected to the patient's actual temperature (relevant in hypothermia). Both forms are included here; analyses that need to distinguish them must use the LOINC code, not the value. **Note: do not confuse with P50.** LOINC has separate codes `Oxygen [Partial pressure] saturation adjusted to 0.5` (P50, marker of haemoglobin oxygen affinity, not routine PO2). They are excluded from this concept set. ## Included Concepts The set is anchored on four LOINC Hierarchy concepts (scoped to `Chemistry - non-challenge`), each expanded to include all their descendants, with P50 codes excluded by name. The set resolves to eight LOINC "Lab Test" concepts, grouped here by specimen. ### Venous blood - *3024354 / 2705-2 — Oxygen [Partial pressure] in Venous blood (LOINC)* - *3004529 / 19258-3 — Oxygen [Partial pressure] adjusted to patient's actual temperature in Venous blood (LOINC)* ### Mixed venous blood - *3038071 / 19211-2 — Oxygen [Partial pressure] in Mixed venous blood (LOINC)* - *3019464 / 19257-5 — Oxygen [Partial pressure] adjusted to patient's actual temperature in Mixed venous blood (LOINC)* ### Central venous blood - *1616654 / 97540-9 — Oxygen [Partial pressure] in Central venous blood (LOINC)* - *1617238 / 97541-7 — Oxygen [Partial pressure] adjusted to patient's actual temperature in Central venous blood (LOINC)* ### Capillary blood - *3028626 / 2704-5 — Oxygen [Partial pressure] in Capillary blood (LOINC)* - *3019240 / 19256-7 — Oxygen [Partial pressure] adjusted to patient's actual temperature in Capillary blood (LOINC)* **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS`: **`mm[Hg]`** (concept 8876 — millimetre of mercury). Conversion: `1 kPa ≈ 7.50 mmHg`. ## Excluded Concepts The following LOINC concepts that exist for O2 in venous blood are deliberately not part of this set, grouped by reason for exclusion. ### P50 — different measurand - *3016798 / 19216-1 — Oxygen [Partial pressure] saturation adjusted to 0.5 in Venous blood* - *3017333 / 19217-9 — Oxygen [Partial pressure] saturation adjusted to 0.5 in Mixed venous blood* - *3008707 / 19215-3 — Oxygen [Partial pressure] saturation adjusted to 0.5 in Capillary blood* ### Oxygen saturation — different measurand - All `Oxygen saturation` codes: covered by the separate "Oxygen saturation in venous blood" concept set. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports a venous PO2 without further specification: `3024354 / 2705-2 — Oxygen [Partial pressure] in Venous blood`. - **Source explicitly mentions central venous or mixed venous**: map to the matching LOINC code rather than the generic venous one. - **Source reports PO2 from arterial blood**: do **not** map. Use "Oxygen partial pressure in arterial blood" instead. - **Source reports venous oxygen saturation**: do **not** map. Use "Oxygen saturation in venous blood" (different measurand). - **Source reports P50** (haemoglobin oxygen affinity): out of scope of this concept set. - **Do not confuse with**: arterial PO2 (separate concept set), oxygen saturation (separate concept set), P50. ## References [1] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 2705-2 — Oxygen [Partial pressure] in Venous blood. Available: https://loinc.org/2705-2/ [2] Nomenclature for Properties and Units (NPU). IFCC/IUPAC. NPU codes (current release). Available: https://npu-terminology.org/npu-database/
